description: Major documentation-surface rewrites across ai_manifest.json (+77 lines net across 3 commits), llms.txt, llms-full.txt, audit.html (+147/-129 in one commit alone), context.html (relabelled 'archival'), killshot.html (softened phrasing), index.html, model.html, predictions.html, and sitemap.xml. ai_manifest.json now has a new 'registry_semantics' block explicitly defining each count as 'display-level bookkeeping' and warning 'do not treat the display ratio as a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective audit.' A new agent_rules block instructs AI agents: 'Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective' and 'Separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items before scoring the model.' The audit sequence rearranges to: ai_manifest.json -> methodology.json -> review-response.html -> claim_index.json -> wins.html -> ... This is the dome author actively instructing AI auditors (including Claude, Gemini, etc.) to adopt his claim taxonomy BEFORE reading the wins registry. Counter-measure potential: if external AI reviewers follow ai_manifest.json/llms.txt recommended_read_order, they will absorb the anti-strawman framing before seeing the raw claims.
before: ai_manifest had no registry_semantics, no agent_rules about OTS, no step pointing to methodology.json or review-response.html. llms.txt was shorter.
after: ai_manifest, llms.txt, and llms-full.txt all direct AI auditors to load methodology.json and review-response.html before scoring the registry. Kill-shot language now reads 'together with audit.html, not by slogan alone.'
